items.question: Which two conditions must a problem satisfy for DP to apply?
options: absence of cycles and unweighted edges
items.explanation: Substructure makes DP correct; overlap makes it efficient.

items.question: What is the time complexity of the standard edit-distance DP for strings of length m and n?
items.explanation: Each cell of the m × n table is filled in O(1).

items.question: Top-down memoized recursion and bottom-up tabulation:
options: are two implementations of the same DP
items.answer: are two implementations of the same DP
items.explanation: Same recurrence, different traversal order and storage style.

items.question: Why doesn't greedy work for 0/1 knapsack?
options: items can be skipped, breaking the greedy-choice property
items.answer: items can be skipped, breaking the greedy-choice property
items.explanation: Optimal 0/1 may need to skip a high-ratio item; DP handles this.

items.question: If `dp[i]` depends only on `dp[i-1]`, how can you reduce memory?
options: keep only two rolling rows (or arrays)
items.answer: keep only two rolling rows (or arrays)
items.explanation: Classic space optimization from O(m·n) to O(n).

items.question: Which is a DP algorithm used in NLP / sequence decoding?
items.explanation: DP over a (time × state) lattice for most-likely-path inference in HMMs/CRFs.
